prompt.content: Explain which organelle produces most cellular energy and why.

completion.content: Cells convert nutrients into ATP mainly through oxidative phosphorylation, which happens across the inner membrane of a dedicated organelle. Because respiration and ATP synthase activity are localized there, the mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell.

answer: the mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell

completion.content: Phosphofructokinase catalyzes the committed, rate-limiting step of glycolysis, converting fructose-6-phosphate with ATP as its substrate; it is allosterically inhibited by ATP and citrate.

info.grading_criteria: Award full credit when the response names the enzyme, cites its substrate, and explains the rate-limiting step.
